components.name: D-Glucose
components.evidence: filter-sterilized glucose was added to each microcosm to achieve a final concentration of 20 mM, simulating root exudates

components.name: Dinitrogen (N2, gas phase)
components.evidence: Nitrogen was omitted by replacing all N-containing salts ... The root-solution system was deoxygenated with N2, sealed with a rubber stopper ... (nitrogen-fixation microcosm; N2 is the sole nitrogen source for the diazotroph)

components.name: Calcium chloride dihydrate (CaCl2.2H2O)
components.role: calcium / chloride salt (N-free replacement of Ca(NO3)2.4H2O)
components.evidence: replacing all N-containing salts (Ca(NO3)2.4H2O, KNO3, and NH4H2PO4) with equimolar amounts of their chloride- or sulfate-based counterparts (CaCl2.2H2O, KH2PO4, and K2SO4). [conc = equimolar to canonical Hoagland Ca(NO3)2 = 4 mM; strength not stated in paper]

components.name: Potassium sulfate (K2SO4)
components.role: potassium / sulfate salt (N-free replacement of KNO3)
components.evidence: replacing all N-containing salts (Ca(NO3)2.4H2O, KNO3, and NH4H2PO4) with equimolar amounts of their chloride- or sulfate-based counterparts (CaCl2.2H2O, KH2PO4, and K2SO4). [conc = equimolar to canonical Hoagland KNO3 = 6 mM; strength not stated in paper]

components.name: Monopotassium phosphate (KH2PO4)
components.role: phosphate / potassium salt (N-free replacement of NH4H2PO4)
components.evidence: replacing all N-containing salts (Ca(NO3)2.4H2O, KNO3, and NH4H2PO4) with equimolar amounts of their chloride- or sulfate-based counterparts (CaCl2.2H2O, KH2PO4, and K2SO4). [conc = equimolar to canonical Hoagland NH4H2PO4 = 1 mM; strength not stated in paper]

components.name: Magnesium sulfate heptahydrate (MgSO4.7H2O)
components.evidence: 20 ml of modified N-free Hoagland solution (pH 6.0). [MgSO4.7H2O is the standard Hoagland Mg source; canonical full-strength = 2 mM. Not the modified N-salt, so retained unchanged; not itemized in paper text.]

components.name: Boric acid (H3BO3)
components.evidence: modified N-free Hoagland solution (pH 6.0). [Standard Hoagland micronutrient stock (1 mL/L): H3BO3 2.86 g/L stock -> 2.86 mg/L final]

components.name: Manganese(II) chloride tetrahydrate (MnCl2.4H2O)
components.evidence: modified N-free Hoagland solution (pH 6.0). [Standard Hoagland micronutrient stock (1 mL/L): MnCl2.4H2O 1.81 g/L stock -> 1.81 mg/L final]

components.name: Zinc sulfate heptahydrate (ZnSO4.7H2O)
components.evidence: modified N-free Hoagland solution (pH 6.0). [Standard Hoagland micronutrient stock (1 mL/L): ZnSO4.7H2O 0.22 g/L stock -> 0.22 mg/L final]

components.name: Copper(II) sulfate pentahydrate (CuSO4.5H2O)
components.evidence: modified N-free Hoagland solution (pH 6.0). [Standard Hoagland micronutrient stock (1 mL/L): CuSO4.5H2O 0.08 g/L stock -> 0.08 mg/L final]

components.name: Molybdic acid monohydrate (H2MoO4.H2O)
components.evidence: modified N-free Hoagland solution (pH 6.0). [Standard Hoagland micronutrient stock (1 mL/L): H2MoO4.H2O 0.02 g/L stock -> 0.02 mg/L final]

components.name: Iron(III)-EDTA chelate (Fe-EDTA / Fe-EDDHA)
components.role: trace element (iron), base Hoagland iron chelate
components.evidence: modified N-free Hoagland solution (pH 6.0). [Standard Hoagland supplies iron as a Fe-EDTA/Fe-EDDHA chelate. Paper does not state iron omission from the base solution for the control; however this is an iron-focused study (CK = no iron plaque on roots) and the medium iron source/level is not explicitly confirmed -> flagged.]

media.base_medium: Modified nitrogen-free Hoagland solution (Hoagland & Arnon 1950), pH 6.0, N-omitted, + 20 mM glucose; anoxic (N2-sparged)

sources_chased.what: Paper main text (Materials and Methods, microcosm setup) — full-text JATS XML
added: N-free salt substitutions CaCl2.2H2O, KH2PO4, K2SO4
removed erroneous NH4+ and NO3- (N was deliberately omitted)

sources_chased.what: Reference 36 (Liu et al. 2019, Soil Biol Biochem) cited for IRRI nutrient solution used to raise seedlings
added: confirmed ref 36 is the seedling IRRI solution, NOT the microcosm Hoagland base recipe; no Hoagland recipe cited

sources_chased.what: Canonical Hoagland solution composition (Hoagland & Arnon 1950) to expand the named base medium
Supplementary files (wrag088_Supplemental_Files); Tables S1 (soil properties), S2 (fermentation products)
sources_chased.how: identified supplementary section in XML — no media recipe table present (S1=soil physicochemistry, S2=metabolites)
added: confirmed no full media-recipe table exists in the supplement

missing_before: Dinitrogen (N2) as the nitrogen source / gas phase
media.notes: The current record wrongly listed Ammonium (nh4) and Nitrate (no3) as components: the paper explicitly OMITTED all nitrogen (this is the N-free nitrogen-fixation control), replacing the three N-salts with CaCl2.2H2O, KH2PO4 and K2SO4 — both were removed. The paper names 'modified N-free Hoagland solution' but cites no Hoagland recipe and gives no trace-element/Mg list, so the base macro/micronutrient salts (Mg, B, Mn, Zn, Cu, Mo, Fe chelate) were expanded from the canonical Hoagland & Arnon (1950) formulation; the exact Hoagland strength used, and whether iron was retained in this no-plaque control, are not stated in the paper (flagged), hence medium confidence. N2 is the sole nitrogen source (diazotrophic fixation).
